creator: steve.jones@codeweavers.net
text:
I cannot get lagg and carp running on a DELL SC1435 with dual interfaces.
Everytime I try and restart the network, I cause a system panic.

kernel customisation: 
	"device carp";
/boot/loader.conf customisation: 
	"if_lagg_load="YES""
rc.conf configuration:
	cloned_interfaces="lagg0 carp0 carp1"
	ifconfig_bge0="up"
	ifconfig_bge1="up"
	ifconfig_lagg0="laggproto failover laggport bge0 laggport bge1
10.1.1.1 netmask 255.255.0.0"
	ifconfig_carp0="inet 10.1.100.1/16 vhid 1 pass passwd"
	ifconfig_carp1="inet 10.1.101.1/16 vhid 2 advskew 64 pass passwd"
dmesg output:

on boot up, the creation of the lagg interface is ignored, the carp
interfaces are created but missing their configuration.
		
however, running /etc/netstart or /etc/rc.d/netif restart causes a system panic:

-- start of system panic
Fatal trap 12: page fault while in kernel mode cpuid=1; apic id = 01
fault virtual address 	= 0xb7
fault code 		= supervisor read data, page not present
instruction pointer	= 0x8: 0xffffffff8053da20
stack pointer 		= 0x10: 0xffffffffae5a3980
frame pointer		= 0x10: 0xffffff000166f570
code segment		= base 0x0, limit 0xfffff, type0x1b
			= DPL 0, pres 1, long 1, def 32 0, gran 1  
processor eflags 	= interrupt enabled, resume, 10PL = 0
current process		= 163 (ifconfig)
trap number		= 12		
panic: page fault
cpuid=1
Uptime: 35m14s
Physical Memory: 2034MB

-- end of system panic
		
This issue is on a DELL SC1435 (AMD Operton), with dual Broadcom NetXtreme
Gigabit Ethernet Controllers.

Fix: 

not known
How-To-Repeat: With the configuration above following a reboot:
     #/etc/rc.d/netif restart
